menu.name: Tuscan White Bean Salad with Za'atar
ingredients: 1 cup dried white beans (cannellini or great northern), yields ~2.5 cups cooked
1/2 teaspoon Aleppo pepper (or red pepper flakes)
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Combine cooked white beans, cucumbers, tomatoes, green onions, parsley, and mint in large bowl
Add lemon zest and season with salt, pepper, za'atar, sumac, and Aleppo pepper
Add lemon juice and olive oil, toss well to combine
Let sit 10 minutes before serving

menu.name: Lentil and Chickpea Salad with Radish and Herbs
ingredients: 1/2 cup dried French green lentils, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
1/2 cup dried chickpeas, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
Salt and black pepper to taste
night_of_steps_list: Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper for dressing
Combine cooked lentils, chickpeas, chopped radishes, mint, and dill in large bowl
Pour dressing over salad and toss well
Serve immediately or chill

menu.name: Crispy Baked Tofu Salad with Tahini Dressing
menu.substitutions: Replace any kale with spinach or mixed greens
night_of_steps_list: Whisk tahini, water, lemon juice, garlic, and salt for dressing; set aside
Mix cornstarch, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper; coat tofu cubes
Heat olive oil in pan, fry tofu 12 minutes, flipping every 2-3 minutes until crispy
Arrange greens in bowl, top with tomatoes, cucumber, onion, and crispy tofu
Drizzle with tahini dressing and serve

menu.name: Mediterranean Kidney Bean Salad with Lemon-Garlic Dressing
ingredients: 1/2 cup dried kidney beans, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
1/2 cup dried chickpeas, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
2 small garlic cloves, minced or grated
night_of_steps_list: Combine cooked kidney beans and chickpeas in large salad bowl
Add diced cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and parsley
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, lemon zest, sugar, salt, and pepper
Pour dressing over salad and toss well to combine
Serve immediately or chill

menu.name: Paneer Salad with Spicy Greek Yogurt Dressing
menu.substitutions: Replace iceberg lettuce with romaine or mixed greens
night_of_steps_list: Blend Greek yogurt, soaked cashews, garlic, spices, lemon juice, maple syrup, and salt until smooth
Heat oil in pan and pan-fry paneer cubes with salt until golden on all sides
Combine lettuce, bell peppers, onions, tomatoes, and cucumber in large bowl
Top with pan-fried paneer and drizzle with yogurt dressing
Toss and serve

menu.name: Edamame Rainbow Salad with Sesame Ginger Dressing
night_of_steps_list: If using frozen edamame, thaw under warm water and drain well
Combine edamame, cabbage, cucumbers, bell peppers, carrots, cilantro, and green onions in large bowl
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, olive oil, maple syrup, ginger, and garlic
Pour dressing over salad and toss to combine
Top with toasted almonds and sesame seeds, serve

menu.name: Green Lentil Salad with Za'atar and Roasted Red Peppers
ingredients: 1/2 cup dried green lentils, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
1/2 cup dried chickpeas, yields ~1.25 cups cooked
night_of_steps_list: Cook lentils in salted boiling water until tender, about 20 minutes; drain and rinse in cold water
Combine cooked lentils, chickpeas, cucumber, roasted red peppers, red onion, and parsley in large bowl
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, garlic, za'atar, salt, and pepper
Pour dressing over salad and toss gently to combine
Serve immediately or chill

saturday: Soak 1 cup dried white beans in water (for Monday) - Saturday 9pm
Soak 1/2 cup dried French green lentils in water (for Tuesday) - Saturday 9pm
Soak 1 cup dried chickpeas in water (for Tuesday and Thursday) - Saturday 9pm
Soak 1/2 cup dried kidney beans in water (for Thursday) - Saturday 9pm
Soak 1 cup dried green lentils in water (for Sunday) - Saturday 9pm
sunday: Cook white beans: drain, add to pot with 2 inches water, boil then simmer 60-90 minutes until tender; drain and cool
Cook French green lentils: drain, add to pot with garlic and olive oil, cover with 4 cups water, boil then simmer 20-35 minutes; drain and cool
Cook chickpeas: drain, add to pot with 2 inches water, boil then simmer 60-90 minutes until tender; drain and cool (divide into 2 portions for Tuesday and Thursday)
Cook kidney beans: drain, add to pot with 2 inches water, boil then simmer 60-90 minutes until tender; drain and cool
Cook green lentils: drain, add to pot with water, boil then simmer 20-30 minutes; drain and cool
Prep vegetables: wash and dry all salad greens, store in containers with paper towels
Chop vegetables: dice cucumbers (store in airtight containers), slice radishes, dice bell peppers
Chop herbs: parsley, mint, dill, cilantro (store separately in damp paper towels in containers)
Make dressing jars: prepare lemon-olive oil dressings for Monday, Thursday, Sunday (store refrigerated)
Toast nuts and seeds: almonds, sesame seeds for Saturday (store in airtight container)
Soak cashews for Friday's dressing (2 hours in warm water)

produce: Mixed greens or spinach (12 cups total)
Red cabbage (1 small head or 2 cups pre-shredded)
dried_beans_legumes: 1 cup dried white beans (cannellini or great northern)
dairy_and_other: Greek yogurt or hung curd (1/2 cup)
pantry_check: Aleppo pepper or red pepper flakes
